skill.name: remembering-conversations
skill.description: Searches past Claude Code conversation memory before making decisions, revisiting prior work, or handling unfamiliar workflows.
skill.summary: Finds relevant past conversations and summarizes prior decisions, patterns, and lessons.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but these are Markdown formatting and prose false positives. The confirmed concern is legitimate but sensitive access to past conversation memory through MCP search and read tools, including conversation files addressed by absolute path.

medium_findings.title: Conversation History Access Requires Privacy Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to search past conversations and use MCP tools that can read archived conversation files by path. This is a legitimate memory workflow, but it may expose sensitive prior user messages, tool outputs, or project details if used without clear task relevance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe searching previous conversations and reading full conversation archives. The behavior is intended, so the risk is privacy exposure rather than confirmed malicious activity.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated Markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby shell execution. The referenced lines document tool names, parameters, and example payloads; no executable script or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Markdown documentation and typed examples. I found no code file, interpreter directive, or executable command path in these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged lines as weak cryptography, but the cited locations contain description text, prompt template fields, headings, and parameter tables. No cryptographic API, hash function call, or security-sensitive algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation, not implementation. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic code in these files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign Text
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged lines as reconnaissance, but the cited text is ordinary guidance and error documentation. No command gathers host, network, user, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain Markdown bullets. I found no evidence of system inspection commands or instructions to enumerate the host environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: MCP Read Tool Accepts Conversation File Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented read tool accepts an absolute path to a JSONL conversation archive. This creates a privacy-sensitive filesystem capability that should be scoped to relevant memory searches and user intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The parameter table and examples explicitly show path-based conversation reads. The risk depends on tool enforcement and usage context, so it is medium confidence rather than a confirmed exploit.

content.value_statement: Teams often repeat decisions because past AI conversations are hard to search. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to find relevant conversation memory before proposing approaches.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when past conversation history is likely useful for the current task.
Directs the assistant to use a dedicated search-conversations subagent for historical lookup.
Defines when to search memory after understanding the current codebase or request.
Documents search parameters for semantic, text, combined, and date-filtered memory lookup.
Documents how to read a selected conversation range for source-backed context.
Encourages concise summaries with actionable insights and sources.
limitations: Requires an episodic-memory MCP plugin and indexed conversation archive to provide results.
Can surface sensitive prior conversation content if memory access is not scoped carefully.
Does not create or maintain the conversation archive itself.
May return incomplete context when older conversations were not indexed or retained.

use_cases.title: Reuse Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Search previous discussions before choosing an implementation approach for a familiar codebase.

use_cases.title: Recover Project Context
use_cases.description: Find why a team selected a pattern, dependency, or workflow during earlier AI-assisted work.

use_cases.title: Avoid Repeating Failed Attempts
use_cases.description: Look up past debugging notes and gotchas before trying another fix for a recurring issue.

prompt_templates.title: Find Related History
prompt_templates.prompt: Search past conversations for decisions related to this task. Summarize the most relevant prior context and cite the sources found.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Prior Approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: After reviewing the current code, search conversation history for similar implementation choices. Compare prior approaches and recommend one for this task.

prompt_templates.title: Recover Rationale
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding why a decision was made
prompt_templates.prompt: Search memory for why we chose the current pattern. Return the decision, rationale, tradeoffs, and any warnings from past conversations.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Historical Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Search past conversations using focused queries for architecture, failures, and constraints. Read the strongest sources and produce an evidence-backed action plan.

output_examples.input: How should we handle authentication errors in this app?
output_examples.output: The assistant searches prior conversations after inspecting the current code, then summarizes earlier authentication decisions, known failure cases, and the recommended path.

output_examples.input: Do you remember why we avoided that dependency?
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the prior discussion, explains the original rationale, lists the tradeoffs, and cites the relevant conversation sources.

output_examples.input: I am stuck on a migration issue we solved before.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches memory for similar migration work, reads the strongest matches, and returns previous fixes plus cautions for this codebase.

best_practices: Search memory after understanding the current task and code context.
Use focused queries that name the project, feature, error, or decision.
Read only the most relevant conversation ranges needed to support the answer.
anti_patterns: Searching memory before understanding the user request or current codebase.
Reading full conversation archives when a small sourced range is enough.
Using old conversation context as authority without checking current files.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an assistant search past AI conversations and summarize relevant decisions before answering a current task.

faq.question: Does it work without the episodic-memory MCP plugin?
faq.answer: No. The skill depends on MCP tools that search and read an indexed conversation archive.

faq.question: Can it access sensitive history?
faq.answer: Yes. It can surface prior conversation content, so users should scope searches to relevant work.

faq.question: Does it replace current code inspection?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to inspect the current task and code before using memory for historical context.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a privacy warning because it can read past conversation archives through MCP tooling.
